title: Clone Graph
description: Given a reference of a node in a connected undirected graph, return a deep copy (clone) of the graph. Each node in the graph contains a value (val) and a list of its neighbors (neighbors). The graph is guaranteed to be non-empty, meaning at least one node exists.

examples.explanation: The original graph consists of 4 nodes with the following connections: Node 1 is connected to Nodes 2 and 4, Node 2 to Nodes 1 and 3, Node 3 to Node 2, and Node 4 to Node 1. The deep copy will have the same structure.
The input graph consists of a single node with no neighbors. The clone will also contain this single node with an empty neighbor list.
The graph consists of two nodes, each connected to the other. The clone will preserve this relationship, resulting in the same structure.

constraints: The number of nodes in the graph is between 0 and 100.
Each node's value is unique and can be between 1 and 100.
The graph is connected and undirected.
hints: Consider using a hash map to keep track of copied nodes.
You can use either DFS or BFS for traversing and cloning the graph.
Make sure to handle cycles in the graph properly to avoid infinite loops.
Think about how to create new nodes while ensuring that the neighbors are correctly set.
